description: A 'nowiso' is the defined place or means through which two things interact. It may be a plug, a control surface, a doorway, or an agreed data exchange. 'norui' draws a boundary and 'lorea' states connection; the interface identifies how and where the interaction can actually occur.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and round into true-mid back 'o'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then unround into stressed high-front 'i'. Raise the tongue blade towards the alveolar ridge for unvoiced 's' and finish with true-mid rounded back 'o'.
sound_symbolism: Rounded 'no' meets the narrowing glide of stressed 'wi', then opens again through hissing 'so'. The mouth crosses a shaped middle and answers on the other side. Both edges remain audible.
usage_notes: The noun is countable. Transfer, signal, or accessibility clauses can state what crosses the interface and whether participants can use it. A screen is one possible interface. Whether the exchange is compatible and mutually understood must still be tested.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The concept attends to the shaped meeting between distinct forms, where connection depends on how edges are made to relate.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: An interface names where interaction can occur; it promises neither understanding nor access.

semantic_domains.communication: an informational interface can mediate exchange without guaranteeing understanding
semantic_domains.creation: a designed means through which systems or components interact
semantic_domains.spatial: a boundary or contact location across which exchange occurs
